skill.name: gws-calendar-insert
skill.description: Create new events in Google Calendar via the Google Workspace CLI.
skill.summary: AI-powered skill for creating Google Calendar events with customizable attendees, locations, and Google Meet integration.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected patterns in SKILL.md (backticks, path references, crypto-like strings) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The file is Markdown documentation, not executable code. Backticks are inline code formatting, path references are relative documentation links, and no actual shell execution occurs. The skill wraps the official gws CLI tool and requires user confirmation before write operations. Single risk factor: external_commands (legitimate CLI invocation).

low_findings.title: External CLI Tool Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires gws CLI binary to be installed and authenticated with Google Workspace
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Explicit dependency on external gws binary documented in metadata section

content.value_statement: Simplify calendar management by letting AI create events through natural language. Schedule meetings, add attendees, and configure Google Meet links without manual form filling.
actual_capabilities: Create new calendar events with title, start time, and end time
Add attendees to events by email address
Generate Google Meet video conference links automatically
Require user confirmation before executing write operations
limitations: Requires gws CLI to be installed and authenticated with Google Workspace
Cannot modify or delete existing events (insert only)
Does not support recurring event creation
Cannot check calendar availability or resolve scheduling conflicts

use_cases.title: Quick Meeting Scheduling
use_cases.description: Create impromptu meetings with minimal input - just provide summary and times
use_cases.target_user: Team leads who frequently schedule standups and check-ins

use_cases.title: Collaborative Event Planning
use_cases.description: Schedule events with multiple attendees and optional Google Meet integration

use_cases.title: Automated Calendar Management
use_cases.description: Integrate calendar creation into AI-assisted workflows for seamless scheduling

prompt_templates.title: Basic Event Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a calendar event titled 'Team Standup' from 9:00 AM to 9:30 AM tomorrow
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick single-argument scheduling for routine events

prompt_templates.title: Meeting with Attendees
prompt_templates.prompt: Schedule a 'Project Review' meeting next Monday at 2 PM with alice@example.com and bob@example.com

prompt_templates.title: Virtual Meeting Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 'Client Call' event on Friday at 10 AM with a Google Meet link and add the client's email

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Event Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Add an 'All-Day Conference' event for June 15th at the Convention Center with description 'Annual tech conference' and send invites to the team
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex events with location, description, and multiple details

output_examples.input: Create a standup meeting tomorrow from 9 to 9:30 AM
output_examples.output: Event 'Standup' created successfully on primary calendar for 2026-06-17T09:00:00-07:00 to 2026-06-17T09:30:00-07:00

output_examples.input: Schedule a review with alice@example.com next week with a Meet link
output_examples.output: Event 'Review' created with attendee alice@example.com and Google Meet conference link added

best_practices: Always confirm event details with the user before executing the insert command
Use RFC3339/ISO 8601 format for all datetime values to avoid timezone confusion
Verify attendee email addresses are valid before adding them to events
anti_patterns: Creating events without explicit user confirmation for the time and attendees
Assuming default calendar when user may have multiple calendars
Scheduling events without checking if the user is already busy at that time

faq.question: What do I need before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need the gws CLI installed and authenticated with your Google Workspace account. Run 'gws generate-skills' to set up required dependencies.

faq.question: Can this skill modify existing calendar events?
faq.answer: No, this skill only creates new events. Use the gws-calendar-update skill for modifying existing events.

faq.question: How do I add multiple attendees to an event?
faq.answer: Use the --attendee flag multiple times, once for each email address you want to invite.

faq.question: Does this skill work with personal Google Calendar accounts?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Google Workspace (business/education) accounts. Personal Gmail accounts may have limited functionality.

faq.question: What happens if I don't specify a calendar?
faq.answer: Events are created in your primary calendar by default. Use --calendar to target a specific calendar by ID.

faq.question: Can I create recurring events with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill creates single events only. Recurring events require additional configuration not currently supported.
